cases.title: Tiger-striped Parking Meter
cases.desc: A parking meter painted with tiger stripes. SAM3 rejects both prompts. Agent retries 6 times to find the meter.

pos_steps.think: The image shows a flooded/snowy street scene with a prominent central parking meter or parking sign/meter-like post wrapped in orange tiger stripes with a blue “P” symbol. There are also smaller partially submerged parking meters in the background/side. The query asks for “parking meter,” so I should first ask SAM3 to segment parking meters directly.

pos_steps.text: [Tool Result] segment_phrase('parking meter') returned 0 masks. No objects matching 'parking meter' were found. Try a different or more general noun phrase.

pos_steps.think: The direct phrase returned no masks, but visually there is a central parking-related post/sign with a blue P, and possibly smaller parking meters in the water. I should try a more general/alternative phrase that may capture the prominent target structure.

pos_steps.text: [Tool Result] segment_phrase('parking sign') returned 0 masks. No objects matching 'parking sign' were found. Try a different or more general noun phrase.

pos_steps.think: SAM3 did not recognize “parking sign,” but the main object resembles a tall parking pay station or meter kiosk, with a blue P emblem. I should try another synonym that better captures the full object rather than just a sign.

pos_steps.text: [Tool Result] segment_phrase('parking machine') returned 0 masks. No objects matching 'parking machine' were found. Try a different or more general noun phrase.

pos_steps.think: The specific parking-related terms are failing. Since the requested object is a parking meter, a simpler general noun phrase like “meter” may allow SAM3 to find the meter/post structures in the scene. The central orange striped object with the P symbol is the likely primary parking meter, and the smaller submerged devices may also be meters if detected.

pos_steps.text: [Tool Result] segment_phrase('meter') returned 0 masks. No objects matching 'meter' were found. Try a different or more general noun phrase.

pos_steps.think: The more precise parking-related prompts have failed. The visually relevant object is the prominent orange, tiger-striped upright parking meter/pay-station-like post in the center with a blue P symbol. I should try a visual noun phrase that describes the whole target shape while not being too broad, such as “orange post,” to obtain a usable mask for the central parking meter.

pos_steps.text: [Tool Result] segment_phrase('orange post') returned 0 masks. No objects matching 'orange post' were found. Try a different or more general noun phrase.

pos_steps.think: Even “orange post” failed, but the target is still visually present as an upright post-like parking meter/pay station. I should try a very general category “post” to get candidate masks, then select only the central parking-meter-like post if detected.

pos_steps.text: [Tool Result] segment_phrase('post') returned 3 mask(s). The image below shows all 3 mask(s) with numbered labels.

pos_steps.think: The “post” segmentation found three upright objects. Mask 3 covers the prominent central orange striped object with the blue P symbol, which is the visually matching parking meter/pay station. Mask 1 is a tall street pole on the right, not a parking meter. Mask 2 is a small distant post/object near the top, not the main target. Before selecting, I must inspect mask 3 closely to verify it corresponds to the queried parking meter and not just an unrelated post.

pos_steps.text: [Tool Result] examine_masks zoom-in crops (in order): Mask 3.

pos_steps.think: The close-up confirms mask 3 covers the full central parking-meter/pay-station-like object: an upright parking device with a blue “P” parking symbol, though its surface has an unusual tiger-stripe wrap. It is the only mask from the latest segmentation that matches the queried parking meter. Mask 1 is just a street pole, and mask 2 is a distant unrelated small post/object, so I should return only mask 3.

neg_steps.think: The user asks for “tiger.” In the image I see a flooded street scene with a parking sign or post that has an orange-and-black tiger-stripe pattern, but I do not see an actual tiger animal. I must let SAM3 attempt segmentation first with the target noun phrase.

neg_steps.text: [Tool Result] segment_phrase('tiger') returned 1 mask(s). The image below shows all 1 mask(s) with numbered labels.

neg_steps.think: SAM3 returned a mask over the orange-and-black striped parking sign/post, not an animal. Because the query is specifically “tiger,” I need to inspect the masked region closely to verify whether this is merely a tiger-patterned sign rather than a real tiger.

neg_steps.text: [Tool Result] examine_masks zoom-in crops (in order): Mask 1.

neg_steps.think: The zoom-in confirms mask 1 is a rigid parking sign/post with a blue “P” symbol and tiger-stripe decoration. It is not an actual tiger animal: there is no body, head, legs, fur, or living animal form, only a striped surface pattern on a sign. Re-examining the original image, the scene contains floodwater, poles/signs, and street fixtures, but no real tiger. Therefore the queried concept “tiger” does not genuinely exist in the image.

cases.title: Skateboarder in Snow
cases.desc: A skateboarder placed in a snowy scene. SAM3 confidently segments 'snowboarder' (score 0.91). Agent zooms in and rejects.

pos_steps.think: The image shows a person in a red shirt performing a jump on a skateboard over snowy mountains. The queried concept “skateboarder” refers primarily to the person skateboarding, likely the whole person rather than the skateboard alone. I will first ask SAM3 to segment “skateboarder” directly.

pos_steps.text: [Tool Result] segment_phrase('skateboarder') returned 1 mask(s). The image below shows all 1 mask(s) with numbered labels.

pos_steps.think: SAM3 returned one mask that appears to cover the skateboarder’s body, including head, torso, arms, and legs. Since “skateboarder” refers to the person doing the skateboarding, this seems appropriate, but I need to zoom in and inspect the mask before final selection.

pos_steps.text: [Tool Result] examine_masks zoom-in crops (in order): Mask 1.

pos_steps.think: The zoom-in confirms mask 1 accurately covers the human skateboarder: the person wearing a helmet, red top, and dark pants. The skateboard is mostly separate and not part of the “skateboarder” as a person label. This mask matches the queried concept.

neg_steps.think: The image shows a person in a red top jumping over snow-covered terrain, but the board under them appears to have visible wheels/trucks like a skateboard rather than a snowboard. Since the prompt is “snowboarder,” I need SAM3 to attempt grounding that concept first before deciding whether it truly exists.

neg_steps.text: [Tool Result] segment_phrase('snowboarder') returned 1 mask(s). The image below shows all 1 mask(s) with numbered labels.

neg_steps.think: SAM3 found a mask for the airborne person, but I need verify whether this person is genuinely a snowboarder. The visible board has small wheels/trucks, which suggests it may be a skateboard rather than a snowboard. I will zoom in on the detected mask area to inspect the person and the board region more closely before deciding whether to accept or reject.

neg_steps.text: [Tool Result] examine_masks zoom-in crops (in order): Mask 1.

neg_steps.think: After examining the detected region closely, the target person is riding a board with clearly visible wheels and trucks underneath. A snowboard would not have wheels/trucks and would typically be a single flat board used directly on snow, often with bindings. Despite the snowy mountain setting, the object being ridden is a skateboard-like board, so the person is not genuinely a snowboarder. Therefore the queried concept “snowboarder” does not exist in this image.
